title: Deploying AWS File Gateway on VMware ESXi to enable hybrid cloud storage with enterprise-grade security and scalability

tasks.title: Task 1: Sign in to AWS Management Console

steps.text: Click on the Open Console button, and you will get redirected to AWS Console in a new browser tab.

On the AWS sign-in page, Leave the Account ID as default. Never edit/remove the 12 digit Account ID present in the AWS Console. Otherwise, you cannot proceed with the lab. Now copy your User Name and Password in the Lab Console to the IAM Username and Password in AWS Console and click on the Sign in button.
Leave the Account ID as default. Never edit/remove the 12 digit Account ID present in the AWS Console. Otherwise, you cannot proceed with the lab.
Now copy your User Name and Password in the Lab Console to the IAM Username and Password in AWS Console and click on the Sign in button.
Once Signed In to the AWS Management Console, Make the default AWS Region as US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1.

tasks.title: Task 2: Download the .ova file from Storage Gateway

steps.text: In this task, we download the .ova file from the AWS Storage Gateway File Gateway page.
Make sure you are in the US East (N. Virginia) Region.
Navigate to Storage gateway by clicking on the Services menu in the top, then click on Storage gateway
Click “Create gateway” .
Task 2: Download the .ova file from Storage Gateway
Enter ‘lab-storage-gateway’ as the gateway name. In the Gateway options, select Amazon S3 File Gateway.
Task 2: Download the .ova file from Storage Gateway
Scroll down to Platform options to Select “VMware ESXi” as the platform.
Download the .ova file .
Click the Download OVF template button to download the .ova file.
In the last step, check the box labeled ‘Confirm Setup Gateway’ before proceeding.
Click Next .
Task 2: Download the .ova file from Storage Gateway

tasks.title: Task 3: Import the .ova file via VMware

steps.text: In this task, we will import the .ova file into VMware.
Open your VMware application. In the top left corner, click “File” and select the “Open” option. Then, browse to and select the downloaded .ova file.
Task 3: Import the .ova file via VMware
Provide the VM name as "storage_gateway" and proceed to import the OVA file.
Click on the blue text that says “Edit virtual machine settings” (right under “Power on this virtual machine”).
Task 3: Import the .ova file via VMware
Select the existing "Hard Disk (SCSI)" from the list on the left and Click the “Add” button at the bottom of the window.This opens the Add Hardware Wizard.
Task 3: Import the .ova file via VMware
Select Hard Disk under Hardware Type and click Next. to select Disk type as SCSI Click next .
Task 3: Import the .ova file via VMware
Select “create a new virtual disk”
Task 3: Import the .ova file via VMware
Enter 60 GB as the disk size , keep the default option Split virtual disk into multiple files, then click Next and Finish.
Task 3: Import the .ova file via VMware
After adding the 60 GB hard disk, it appears in the device list. Now, click "OK" to save the changes.
Task 3: Import the .ova file via VMware
Click to start “Power on this virtual machine”
Task 3: Import the .ova file via VMware
Once the VM has started, log in by typing the username "admin".
Task 3: Import the .ova file via VMware
Provide the following credentials:
Password: password
UNIX Password: password
New Password: lab@123
Retype New Password: lab@123
Task 3: Import the .ova file via VMware
Copy the IP address.
Task 3: Import the .ova file via VMware

tasks.title: Task 4: Create Amazon S3 File Gateway

steps.text: In this task, we will set up the complete configuration of the S3 File Storage Gateway.

Go to AWS Storage Gateway. Since we have already completed Step 1, verify that you are currently on Step 2. Paste the local IP address of the VM (storage-gateway-vm) without making any changes, then click Next.
Task 4: Create Amazon S3 File Gateway
Click Activate Gateway.
Task 4: Create Amazon S3 File Gateway

Now, we have previously added a 60 GB hard disk to the local VM, which is showing on the portal. This disk acts as a cache. Next, select Deactivate Logging in the CloudWatch Log Group, choose No Alarm in CloudWatch Alarm.
Task 4: Create Amazon S3 File Gateway
Note: Wait for 2 to 5 minutes for the local VM hard disk to appear in the portal. If it doesn’t show up, your VM’s network connection may not be stable or properly configured.
Task 4: Create Amazon S3 File Gateway
Then, click Configure.
Congratulations! We have successfully created the S3 File Gateway.
Task 4: Create Amazon S3 File Gateway

tasks.title: Task 5: Create an S3 bucket and configure it for file sharing

steps.text: In this task, we create an S3 bucket and add a file share to the Storage Gateway.
Navigate to the Services menu at the top and click on S3 in the Storage section.
On the S3 dashboard, click on the Create bucket button and fill in the bucket details.
Select Region as N.Virginia to create an S3 bucket.
Bucket type Select : General purpose
Bucket name: Enter whiz-bucket-<RANDOM_NUMBER>
Task 5: Create an S3 bucket and configure it for file sharing
Object ownership: Select ACLs disabled (recommended) option.
Leave other settings as default.
Click on the Create bucket button.
Go to the Storage Gateway service, click on Gateways, and on the right side, select Create File Share.
Task 5: Create an S3 bucket and configure it for file sharing
Select the recently created gateway (lab-storage-gateway), choose the file share protocol as SMB, select the S3 bucket you created earlier, and set User Authentication to Guest access.
Task 5: Create an S3 bucket and configure it for file sharing
In the User Authentication section, click Configure on the right side, set the password to lab@123, then click Save and Create file share.
Task 5: Create an S3 bucket and configure it for file sharing
After clicking on Save, Scroll down and click on Customize configuration.
Task 5: Create an S3 bucket and configure it for file sharing
Click on Next, Go to Connect S3. In the IAM Role Section, choose Fileshare_iam_role_<RANDOM-NUMBER>
Task 5: Create an S3 bucket and configure it for file sharing
Click on Next. If you see any pop-up message for not setting password, Go to User Access and set the password again and Save.
Leave everything as default, Click Next. Finally, Click Create.

Once the file share is created, it may take few minutes for the status to change to "Available". During this process, do not disconnect or change your Wi-Fi connection, as it can affect the entire lab setup.
Task 5: Create an S3 bucket and configure it for file sharing
Scroll down to copy the Windows mount command
E.g., net use [WindowsDriveLetter]: \\192.168.137.129\whiz-bucket-gateway.
Task 5: Create an S3 bucket and configure it for file sharing
On your computer, open Command Prompt and run it as Administrator.
Task 5: Create an S3 bucket and configure it for file sharing
Paste the mount command, replace [WindowsDriveLetter] with w, and run the command.
Eg : net use w: \\192.168.137.129\whiz-bucket-gateway
User name : smbguest
Password : lab@123
Click Enter
Task 5: Create an S3 bucket and configure it for file sharing
You should now see the message: "The command completed successfully."
Task 5: Create an S3 bucket and configure it for file sharing
Press Win + E to open Windows Explorer, then open the mounted storage.

If you couldn't see the mounted Storage, Go to the search box in the Windows Explorer, Run the command which we copied before. If you receive any network credential detail, Enter the username and password we used in step 4.
Task 5: Create an S3 bucket and configure it for file sharing
Now, go to the S3 bucket you created (e.g., whiz-bucket-storage-gateway), open it, and you will see the folder you created. Congratulations! You have successfully completed the process.
Task 5: Create an S3 bucket and configure it for file sharing
Once the lab steps are completed, please click on the Validation button on the Right side panel.
This will validate the resources in the AWS account and shows you whether you have completed this lab successfully or not.
Sample Output :
Task 7: Validation of the Lab
You have successfully deployed the Amazon S3 File Gateway on a VMware VM.
You created and configured an S3 bucket for file sharing.
You mounted the file share on a local Windows machine and verified access.
You demonstrated seamless integration between on-premises storage and AWS cloud.
Sign out of AWS Account.
You have successfully completed the lab.
Once you have completed the steps, click on End Lab from your lab lab console and wait till the process gets completed.
